title: hemionitis glutinosa
intro: Hemionitis glutinosa, also known as the sticky fern or golden-clawed fern, is a species of fern native to Asia, characterized by its shiny, golden-yellow sporangia and distinctive glabrous stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Hemionitis glutinosa are said to provide several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain and inflammation, and supporting immune function. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, often combined with other herbs, to create teas or tonics for medicinal purposes.
